Create a new conda environment with Python 3.10.13 using: conda create -n tf_py310 python=3.10.13 -y
Activate the new environment: conda activate tf_py310
Install TensorFlow 2.13.0 (or 2.13.10 if available) via pip using the official link: pip install tensorflow==2.13.0
Install TensorFlow Model Garden nightly package: pip install tf-models-nightly
Download the TensorFlow Model Garden vision image classification notebook from https://www.tensorflow.org/tfmodels/vision/image_classification by clicking the 'Download notebook' icon
Open the downloaded notebook in VSCode or Jupyter Notebook inside the conda environment
In the first code cell, import matplotlib.pyplot as plt and run plt.plot([1, 2, 3], [4, 5, 6]) followed by plt.show() to confirm pyplot plotting works before importing tensorflow_models
Add the import statement for tensorflow_models (e.g., import tensorflow_models) or the specific model import line used in the notebook
After importing tensorflow_models, run the same matplotlib plotting commands (plt.plot([1, 2, 3], [4, 5, 6]) and plt.show()) in a new cell
Observe that the plot does not render or display after importing tensorflow_models, indicating pyplot is not working
Check matplotlib backend before importing tensorflow_models by running: import matplotlib; print(matplotlib.get_backend()); record the output (expected to be an interactive backend like 'TkAgg')
Check matplotlib backend after importing tensorflow_models by running the same backend check command; observe it changes to 'agg' (non-interactive backend)

Attempt to set matplotlib backend to 'TkAgg' after importing tensorflow_models using: import matplotlib; matplotlib.use('TkAgg'); import matplotlib.pyplot as plt; run a plot and plt.show() again; verify that the plot still does not display

Restart the environment/kernel and set matplotlib backend to 'TkAgg' before importing tensorflow_models by running: import matplotlib; matplotlib.use('TkAgg'); import tensorflow_models; import matplotlib.pyplot as plt; run a plot and plt.show(); verify that the plot displays correctly
Assert that matplotlib backend is 'TkAgg' after setting it before tensorflow_models import and that the plot renders successfully
Summarize that importing tensorflow_models changes matplotlib backend to 'agg' causing plots not to render unless backend is set before import
